title: 10 best herbal creams for peripheral arterial occlusive disease

remedy_properties: Ginkgo biloba: Improves blood flow by increasing nitric oxide production in blood vessels.
Turmeric (Curcuma longa): Reduces inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to PAOD development.
Boswellia serrata: Inhibits the production of inflammatory compounds, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with PAOD.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because they improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the affected areas. The extracts present in Ginkgo biloba creams, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, have vasodilatory properties, which help to widen blood vessels and increase oxygen delivery to the tissues. This leads to a reduction in symptoms associated with peripheral arterial occlusive disease, including pain and fatigue in the legs. Regular application can promote healing and alleviate discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This terpene helps to improve blood flow and reduce platelet aggregation, which contributes to the reduction of symptoms associated with peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): These phenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect blood vessels from damage and promote vascular health.

Ginkgolides (specifically, ginkgolide A and B): These terpene lactones have anti-inflammatory and anti-platelet effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow in affected areas.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can improve blood flow and reduce symptoms of the disease.
Seeds: They are also used as they contain flavonoids and bilobalide, which have been shown to improve blood circulation and reduce oxidative stress.

Barks: They are sometimes used due to their flavonoid content, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can benefit individuals with peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees with green leaves in late summer.
Dry the leaves in a well-ventilated area at 30-40°c for 3-7 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 2-5% of the ginkgo biloba powder with a moisturizing base such as aloe vera or coconut oil.
Stir the mixture until the powder is fully incorporated and the cream has a uniform consistency.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St. John's Wort's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in affected arteries, improving blood flow and reducing symptoms of PAOD.

Anti-inflammatory activity: The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, decrease inflammation in the affected areas, which can improve circulation and alleviate pain and discomfort associated with PAOD.

Vasodilatory effects: St. John's Wort's vasodilatory properties help to relax blood vessels, increasing blood flow and oxygenation to affected areas, thus reducing symptoms of PAOD such as cramping, fatigue, and coldness in the legs.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort, creams helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because it contains hyperforin, a bioactive compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the affected blood vessels, promoting improved blood flow and tissue oxygenation. Additionally, hyperforin has been shown to improve microcirculation, which can help to alleviate symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease, such as pain and fatigue in the affected limbs.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, thereby improving blood flow and reducing symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

N-O-Methylhyperforin (N-O-MH): N-O-MH has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the blood vessels, thereby improving blood flow and reducing symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Hypericin: Hypericin has anti-inflammatory and vasoconstrictive properties, which help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the affected areas, thereby reducing symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in hyperoside, a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which helps to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow.
Flowers: They contain hyperforin, a naphthodianthron derivative with vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to improve circulation and reduce pain.
Stems: They are a good source of hyperforin and can help to improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms associated with peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves in the early morning when the dew is still present on them.
Dry the collected plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for 1 to 2 weeks.
Steep 100 grams of the dried plant material in 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil for 2 to 3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids.
Mix 100 grams of the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of emulsifying wax in a double boiler to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense cream's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and pain in affected areas, improving circulation and relieving symptoms of PAOD.
Oxidative stress reduction: Frankincense's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress, which can damage blood vessels and worsen PAOD symptoms.

Wound healing enhancement: Frankincense cream's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties promote wound healing by reducing bacteria growth, promoting tissue repair, and improving collagen synthesis, all of which are beneficial for treating ulcers and wounds associated with PAOD.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata, also known as frankincense, creams helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties. The boswellic acids present in these creams have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the affected areas. This leads to reduced pain and improved mobility in patients suffering from peripheral arterial occlusive disease. The cream's ability to reduce inflammation and promote blood flow helps to alleviate symptoms and improve overall quality of life for individuals with this condition.
remedy_constituents: Acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA): AKBA has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the affected areas of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Boswellic acids (specifically, 3-acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid and 11-keto-β-boswellic acid): These boswellic acids have anti-inflammatory and anti-platelet aggregation properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent blood clots from forming in the affected arteries.

Neolignans (specifically, 3-acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid and 11-keto-β-boswellic acid): Neolignans have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the affected areas of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Boswellia serrata roots are used to make creams for peripheral arterial occlusive disease because they are rich in boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties that help improve blood circulation.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and combine 200g of distilled water with 50g of gum resin from boswellia serrata in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to 1 hour to facilitate the extraction process.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a separate container to remove the resin solids.
Add 10g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter to the strained mixture and melt over low heat.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and allow it to cool and solidify for about 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Salicin content: White willow cream contains salicin, a natural derivative of aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to affected areas, thereby alleviating symptoms of PAOD.

Analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties: The herbal cream's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties can help to relieve pain, swelling, and discomfort associated with PAOD, making it an effective alternative or adjunct treatment to traditional medications.
Vasodilatory effects: White willow extract has been shown to dilate blood vessels, which can help improve circulation and reduce the risk of further occlusion or blockage in affected arteries.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salix alba, also known as white willow, creams helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compound, salicin, works by reducing inflammation and pain associated with the condition. By alleviating symptoms and promoting blood flow, Salix alba cream may help improve circulation and alleviate discomfort in the affected limbs. Additionally, its analgesic properties help to manage pain, making it a valuable complementary treatment for individuals suffering from peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: A glycoside compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the affected areas, thereby improving blood flow and reducing symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
Tannins: A type of polyphenol that possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect blood vessels from oxidative damage and promote healthy blood flow to the extremities.

Salicortin: A triterpenoid saponin that has been shown to have vasodilatory effects, helping to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow to the affected areas, thereby reducing symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Salix alba leaves are used to make creams for peripheral arterial occlusive disease due to their flavonoid content, which has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that promote wound healing and improve circulation.

Buds: Salix alba buds are used to make creams for peripheral arterial occlusive disease due to their salicylate content, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of salix alba leaves and bark in the morning when the dew is still present.
Dry the harvested salix alba in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Combine the dried salix alba with 100ml of olive oil in a heat-resistant glass container.
Infuse the mixture in a double boiler at 40°c for 2 hours then strain it.
Mix the infused oil with 50g of beeswax and 20ml of vitamin e oil then heat it until the wax melts.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginseng creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to PAOD by damaging blood vessels and reducing blood flow.

Vasodilatory properties: The active compounds in ginseng creams can help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and oxygen delivery to the affected areas, thereby alleviating symptoms of PAOD such as pain and cramping.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginseng creams contain anti-inflammatory agents that help reduce inflammation in the affected arteries, which can contribute to PAOD by causing further damage and narrowing of blood vessels.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng, also known as ginseng, creams helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because of its vasodilatory properties. The active compounds in Panax ginseng, such as ginsenosides, help to relax blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing inflammation. This increase in blood flow and oxygen delivery to the affected areas can help alleviate symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease, including pain, cramping, and fatigue. Regular use of Panax ginseng creams may also promote healing and improve overall circulation.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: Ginsenosides, particularly ginsenoside Rb1 and Rg1, have vasodilatory effects, which can help improve blood flow and reduce the severity of peripheral arterial occlusive disease symptoms.

Saponins: Saponins in Panax ginseng, such as ginsenoside Rb1, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the affected blood vessels, contributing to improved peripheral circulation.

Terpenoids (specifically, sesquiterpenes): Terpenoids, including sesquiterpenes like panaxynol, have been shown to have vasodilatory effects, improve blood flow, and reduce vascular resistance, which can be beneficial for patients with peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in ginsenosides, which are believed to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation.
Barks: Contain ginsenosides and other compounds that help to promote blood flow and reduce oxidative stress.
Leaves: Have been used in traditional medicine to improve blood circulation and reduce symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_recipe: Extract 4-6 grams of dried panax ginseng root in 500ml of distilled water for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container to separate solids and liquid.
Combine 100g of beeswax with 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler and melt the mixture.
Add 10-15ml of the ginseng extract to the melted beeswax mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the cream mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and set for 30 minutes to 1 hour.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Hawthorn's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in blood vessels, which can contribute to the development of PAOD.

Vasodilatory effects: Hawthorn's flavonoids and other bioactive compounds relax and dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the affected areas and helping to alleviate symptoms of PAOD such as pain, cramping, and discoloration.

Antithrombotic properties: Hawthorn's saponins and flavonoids have been shown to inhibit platelet aggregation and reduce thrombus formation, which can help prevent further blockages in blood vessels already affected by PAOD.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna, also known as hawthorn, creams helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because of its vasodilatory properties. The flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins present in Crataegus monogyna may help to relax and dilate blood vessels, thereby improving blood flow to the extremities. Additionally, hawthorn creams may have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected areas, ultimately alleviating symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease. This natural remedy may offer a promising alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have been shown to have vasodilatory effects, which help to improve blood flow and reduce the severity of symptoms associated with peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Triterpenes: Triterpenes in Crataegus monogyna creams have been found to possess antioxidant properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, contributing to improved blood flow and reduced disease symptoms.

Procyanidins: Procyanidins have been demonstrated to have anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory effects, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow in the affected areas, alleviating symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are used to make creams for peripheral arterial occlusive disease due to their flavonoid content, which may help improve blood flow.
Leaves: They are used to make creams for peripheral arterial occlusive disease due to their antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and improving circulation.
Barks: They are used to make creams for peripheral arterial occlusive disease due to their flavonoid content, which may help improve blood vessel function and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves and flowers of the medicinal plant in early morning when dew is still present on the leaves.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area at a temperature not exceeding 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Extract the medicinal properties of the dried plant material using a solvent such as ethanol in a ratio of 1 part plant to 2 parts solvent.
Mix the extracted herbal liquid with a base oil such as sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1 part herbal liquid to 4 parts base oil.
Whip the mixture using a hand mixer for 10 to 15 minutes until it forms a smooth and creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation and swelling in affected areas, improving blood flow and alleviating symptoms of PAOD.

Antioxidant effects: Antioxidants present in the purple coneflower cream can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and damage to blood vessels, potentially slowing down the progression of PAOD.

Vasodilatory properties: The cream's vasodilatory properties may help relax blood vessel walls, improving blood flow and reducing symptoms such as pain, numbness, or weakness in the legs associated with PAOD.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, creams helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce swelling and improve blood flow to the affected areas. The active compounds in Echinacea, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, may also have antioxidant and vasodilatory effects, which can help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation. These effects can help to alleviate symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease, such as pain and fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and improve blood flow in patients with peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Cichoric acid: As a phenolic compound, cichoric acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect blood vessels from oxidative damage and promote healing in affected areas.

Icariin: A flavonoid glycoside found in Echinacea purpurea, icariin has been shown to have vasodilatory effects, which may help improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure in patients with peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in antioxidants and contain echinacoside, a compound that may help alleviate symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation.

Roots: They are a good source of alkylamides, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help in reducing pain and improving circulation in patients with peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
Stems: They contain a high amount of polysaccharides, which may help in increasing blood flow and reducing inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh echinacea purpurea roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm oven at 50c for 4 hours to preserve their potency.
Combine 100g of dried echinacea purpurea roots with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60c for 2 hours to infuse the oil with the herb's medicinal properties.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and add 20g of beeswax to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal ginger creams can help reduce inflammation in the affected areas, which is a common symptom of peripheral arterial occlusive disease (PAOD). This anti-inflammatory effect helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with PAOD.

Antioxidant property: Ginger's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development and progression of PAOD. By reducing oxidative stress, herbal ginger creams may help prevent further damage to blood vessels.

Vasodilatory property: Herbal ginger creams have been shown to dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to affected areas and reducing symptoms associated with PAOD such as pain, cramping, and numbness. This vasodilation effect helps improve overall circulation and reduces the risk of complications.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because of its anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties. The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to increase blood flow and reduce inflammation in the affected areas. This can help alleviate symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease, including pain and cramping in the legs and feet. By improving circulation, ginger creams may also promote wound healing and reduce the risk of further complications. Regular use may also improve overall quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Gingerol: Gingerol, a phenolic compound, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the affected areas, thereby alleviating symptoms of PAOD.

Shogaol: Shogaol, a phenolic compound similar to gingerol, has vasodilatory effects, which help to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow to the extremities, thereby reducing pain and discomfort associated with PAOD.

Zingerone: Zingerone, a ginger-derived terpene, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to protect blood vessels from damage and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of PAOD.
remedy_parts: Roots: Utilized for their similar content of active compounds to rhizomes, which can aid in reducing pain and inflammation associated with peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
Buds: Employed for their potential to provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits, although less information is available on the specific use of ginger buds in creams for this condition.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes with a sharp knife and store them in a cool dry place.
Peel and finely grate 200g of the rhizomes to release their active compounds for extraction.
Combine 50g of the grated rhizome with 100ml of distilled water in a saucepan and heat it gently.
Steep the mixture for 20 minutes to allow the rhizome's bioactive properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and whip 200g of heavy cream with 10g of the herbal extract until stiff peaks form.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: English lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help reduce inflammation in the affected areas, which can alleviate symptoms of PAOD like pain, swelling, and cramping.

Antioxidant property: Lavender's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that damage blood vessels, reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy vessel function, which is essential for maintaining blood flow to the legs and feet.

Vasodilatory property: The vasodilating compounds in lavender cream, such as linalool, help relax blood vessels, improving blood circulation and reducing symptoms of PAOD like leg cramps, pain, and fatigue.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because of its vasodilatory properties. The active compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow. This can help to reduce symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease, including pain and weakness in the legs. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of lavender may also contribute to its beneficial effects on the affected areas. This can lead to improved circulation and overall quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties, which can help improve blood flow and reduce pain in patients with peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
Linalyl acetate: This ester has a vasodilatory effect, which can help increase blood flow to the affected areas and alleviate symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, thereby improving blood flow and alleviating symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties to help alleviate symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
Flowers: Used for their essential oil, which contains compounds with vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects that may help improve blood flow and reduce pain.

Seeds: Used for their oil, which has been reported to have vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties, potentially helping to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in patients with peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers that are free of contaminants at the peak of potency.
Combine the dried flowers with 300ml of distilled water in a heat-proof container to create a plant infusion.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler or a glass jar placed in a saucepan of simmering water for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl to separate the liquid from the solids.
Blend 50g of emulsifying wax and 50g of beeswax with 20ml of the plant infusion and 20ml of jojoba oil in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Maypop cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling, pain, and stiffness in affected limbs, which can alleviate symptoms of PAOD such as cramping and fatigue.
Antioxidant properties: Maypop's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage blood vessels and exacerbate PAOD, thereby promoting healthier vessel walls and improving circulation.
Vasodilatory properties: Maypop cream's vasodilators help widen narrowed or blocked arteries, increasing blood flow to the affected areas and relieving symptoms of PAOD such as pain and weakness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as maypop, creams helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because it contains flavonoids and other plant compounds that help relax blood vessels and improve circulation. The antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties of Passiflora incarnata may help reduce spasms in blood vessels and alleviate pain associated with peripheral arterial occlusive disease. Additionally, it may help improve symptoms such as cramping, fatigue, and weakness by promoting better blood flow to the affected areas. This may contribute to improved overall health and quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Passiflora incarnata may help with peripheral arterial occlusive disease by exerting vasodilatory effects, improving blood flow, and reducing oxidative stress.

Alkaloids (Harmane): Harmane, an alkaloid found in Passiflora incarnata, may help with peripheral arterial occlusive disease by relaxing vascular smooth muscle and improving blood vessel function, ultimately reducing symptoms of peripheral artery disease.

Fernonol: Fernonol, a lignan found in Passiflora incarnata, may help with peripheral arterial occlusive disease by possessing antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with peripheral artery disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help improve circulation and reduce inflammation.
Roots: The roots are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow.
Seeds: The seeds contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and improve circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves from a trusted source.
Steep 2 cups of the harvested flowers in 4 cups of boiling water for 20 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Mix 1 cup of the strained liquid with 1 cup of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture constantly for 10 minutes then remove from heat and let cool for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ironwort creams helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because they contain bioactive compounds that exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties. These compounds, found in high concentrations in the cream, help to reduce inflammation and promote blood flow in affected areas. By increasing blood circulation, ironwort creams can alleviate symptoms such as pain, cramping, and numbness in the legs, feet, and toes commonly associated with peripheral arterial occlusive disease. This natural approach can provide relief from the discomfort and disability caused by this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities: Ironwort creams contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in affected arteries, which can contribute to the progression of PAOD.

Vasodilatory properties: The herbal extracts in ironwort creams have been shown to dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the affected limbs and reducing symptoms such as pain, cramping, and fatigue associated with PAOD.

Antithrombotic effects: Ironwort creams may also help prevent blood clot formation by inhibiting platelet aggregation and thrombin activity, which can reduce the risk of thromboembolic events in patients with PAOD.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ironwort plant contain bioactive compounds that help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, making them effective in treating peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of ironwort have been shown to possess anti-platelet properties, which can help to prevent blood clots from forming and reduce the risk of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_recipe: Collect dried sideritis scardica flowers at the rate of 200g per 100ml of base cream and store them in an airtight container.
Combine 50ml of sweet almond oil with 20ml of beeswax and heat them gently over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until melted.
Add the dried sideritis scardica flowers to the warm oil mixture and stir well allowing it to steep for 24 hours.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding the solids and squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Pour the filtered herbal infusion into sterilized glass jars filling them to within 1cm of the top and seal immediately allowing it to cool and set at room temperature for 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and improve blood flow to affected areas. The cream's soothing and calming effects can also provide relief from pain, cramping, and numbness associated with PAD. Additionally, lemon balm's antioxidant compounds may help to protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by plaque buildup in peripheral arteries, promoting healthier vessel function and overall circulation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help reduce inflammation in affected limbs, which can alleviate pain and improve blood flow.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant-rich composition of lemon balm cream protects against oxidative stress and damage to the blood vessels, promoting healthy vessel function and reducing the risk of further occlusion.

Vasodilatory properties: Lemon balm's vasodilatory effects can help relax narrowed or constricted blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing symptoms such as claudication (pain when walking) in PAOD patients.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the roots of lemon balm help reduce inflammation and promote blood circulation, making it an ideal choice for creams addressing peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
Rhyzomes: As a natural source of glycosides, rhyzomes from lemon balm contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can be extracted to create creams that soothe and relax blood vessels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves, ensuring they are free from contaminants and pesticides.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator or oven on low heat for 2 hours.
Create a tincture by steeping 20 grams of dried melissa officinalis in 100ml of vodka for 14 days, shaking daily.
Combine 10% w/w of the tincture with 90% w/w of a skin-friendly cream base and heat to 60°c for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool, then fill and seal jars for storage at room temperature for up to 6 months, allowing it to mature for 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea creams helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because they contain a blend of herbs that work synergistically to improve blood flow, reduce inflammation, and relax blood vessels. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in these herbal teas help to protect against oxidative stress and damage to the endothelium, promoting healthy blood vessel function. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of the herbs can help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with PAD, making tea creams a natural and effective adjunctive therapy for managing symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal teas rich in antioxidants, such as green tea or hibiscus tea, help combat oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to PAOD by protecting blood vessels from damage.

Vasodilatory properties: Herbal teas containing compounds like caffeine (from green tea), theobromine (from cacao), or berberine (from Berberis aristata) can help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to affected areas and reducing symptoms of PAOD.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal teas with anti-inflammatory compounds like ginger, turmeric, or rosemary may help reduce inflammation in the affected blood vessels, which can contribute to PAOD by promoting blood vessel constriction and reducing blood flow.
remedy_parts: Barks: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help improve blood flow and reduce pain associated with peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality green tea leaves of camellia sinensis and dry them thoroughly for optimal potency.
Grind dried green tea leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, measuring out 20 grams per batch.
Mix the ground green tea powder with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil and 10 milliliters of beeswax in a heat-resistant glass bowl, stirring until well combined.
Place the mixture in a double boiler or a makeshift one using a glass bowl over a pot of simmering water, heating it to 60°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and solidify at room temperature, which takes approximately 2 hours, before whipping it into a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort creams helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because of its unique ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The cream's active compounds, such as madecassoside and asiaticoside, have been shown to dilate blood vessels and increase oxygen delivery to the affected areas. This can help alleviate symptoms such as cramping, numbness, and tingling in the legs and feet associated with peripheral arterial occlusive disease. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and pain in the affected limbs.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Asiatic Pennywort creams have antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected blood vessels, thereby improving blood flow and reducing symptoms of PAOD.
Anti-inflammatory activity: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate pain, swelling, and discomfort associated with PAOD by reducing inflammation in the affected areas.

Improves microcirculation: Asiatic Pennywort creams have been shown to improve microcirculation, which is essential for maintaining healthy blood flow in peripheral arteries. This can help reduce symptoms such as intermittent claudication, cramping, and numbness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the affected areas.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that aid in reducing pain and swelling associated with peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 100g of fresh centella asiatica leaves, allowing for optimal extraction of bioactive compounds.
Grind the dried centella asiatica into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, taking approximately 30 minutes.
Mix 20g of the ground centella asiatica powder with 50g of cold-pressed coconut oil in a glass beaker, allowing for a 4-hour infusion period.
Blend the infused mixture with 10g of beeswax, 5g of shea butter, and 2g of vitamin e oil using an immersion blender, taking approximately 5 minutes.
Pour the cream mixture into sterile glass containers, filling to the brim, and allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature within 1 hour before storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry creams helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because they contain potent antioxidants and flavonoids that help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. The anthocyanins in blueberries have been shown to increase nitric oxide production, which dilates blood vessels and improves circulation. Additionally, the omega-3 fatty acids and other nutrients in herbal blueberry creams help to reduce oxidative stress and promote healthy vessel walls, alleviating symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease such as pain, cramping, and weakness in the legs.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Blueberries' high antioxidant capacity helps protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to PAOD.

Vasodilatory effects: The anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds in blueberries may help relax blood vessels, improve blood flow, and reduce peripheral resistance, which is beneficial for people with PAOD.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Blueberry extracts have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the affected blood vessels, thereby improving overall circulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, blueberry roots help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
Barks: The bark of the blueberry plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to improve blood flow and alleviate symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_recipe: Collect and dry 50 grams of fresh vaccinium myrtillus berries and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered vaccinium myrtillus with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in a glass jar, stirring until a smooth paste forms.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil to the mixture, heating it at 65 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to create a consistent texture.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify completely before whipping it with an electric mixer to incorporate air and increase its volume.
Transfer the herbal cream to sterilized glass jars, labeling them with the date of preparation and storage instructions to ensure freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English oak creams helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because it contains a unique combination of herbal extracts that work synergistically to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The active compounds in the cream, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to dilate blood vessels and increase oxygen delivery to affected areas, reducing symptoms of claudication and improving overall quality of life for individuals with peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: English Oak's astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing inflammation and improving circulation in affected areas, thereby alleviating PAOD symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in English Oak cream help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development and progression of PAOD.
Anti-inflammatory properties: English Oak's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and pain associated with PAOD, promoting a faster recovery and improved overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, oak roots help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in PAOD.
Barks: Containing triterpenes and phenolic compounds, oak barks exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which alleviate symptoms of PAOD.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried quercus robur bark and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered bark with 100 milliliters of olive oil in a double boiler, heating it gently over low heat for 30 minutes.
Stir in 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil until fully incorporated, increasing the heat to medium for an additional 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping it with a hand mixer for 5 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterile tubes or jars and store them in a cool, dark place for at least two weeks to allow for maturation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yew creams helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because they contain extracts rich in flavonoids, terpenes, and other bioactive compounds that promote vasodilation and improve blood flow to affected areas. These herbal compounds help relax blood vessels, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to the narrowing of arteries and reduced blood flow associated with peripheral arterial occlusive disease. By increasing blood flow, yew creams may help alleviate symptoms such as pain, cramping, and numbness in the legs and feet.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Yew extracts have been shown to possess antioxidant properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, potentially reducing the progression of PAOD.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Yew extracts have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling and inflammation in affected arteries, thereby improving blood flow and alleviating symptoms of PAOD.

Cardiovascular protective properties: Some studies suggest that Yew extracts may exhibit cardiovascular protective effects by reducing the risk of atherosclerosis (plaque buildup in arteries), which can contribute to PAOD.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain alkaloids like taxine, which helps to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation, making it beneficial for treating peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
Barks: The barks have been traditionally used to treat circulatory disorders, including peripheral arterial occlusive disease, due to their anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried leaves of taxus baccata and store them in an airtight container away from direct sunlight.
Combine one part dried taxus baccata leaves with two parts coconut oil and infuse the mixture for six hours under room temperature.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material and transfer it to a clean glass jar.
Add 5% vitamin e oil and 2% beeswax to the mixture, stirring until the wax is fully incorporated and the mixture has reached a smooth consistency.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken for at least two hours before transferring it to airtight containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation and improves circulation. By reducing inflammation in affected arteries, turmeric cream may help alleviate symptoms such as pain, cramping, and weakness in the legs and feet. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and damage to blood vessels, promoting healthy vessel function and potentially slowing disease progression. This natural approach can complement traditional treatments and improve overall quality of life for individuals with peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, reduces inflammation in the affected areas, which helps to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with PAOD.
Antioxidant property: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage blood vessels and worsen PAOD, promoting vascular health and preventing further damage.

Vasodilatory property: Turmeric's active compounds, including curcumin and other polyphenols, dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, which helps to reduce symptoms of PAOD such as claudication (pain with walking) and rest pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of turmeric contain high amounts of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Roots: Turmeric roots are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the body, making them effective for treating peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric rhizomes of curcuma longa and wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Grind the dried turmeric rhizomes into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 minutes.
Mix one part powdered turmeric with two parts coconut oil in a double boiler and heat gently for 1 hour.
Add beeswax and vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir until they melt completely in 20 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and let it cool at room temperature before storing in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail creams helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because of their unique ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The herb's high concentration of silicates, flavonoids, and phenolic acids work together to relax constricted blood vessels and increase oxygenation to the affected limbs. Additionally, field horsetail's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with PAD, allowing for improved mobility and quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities: Field Horsetail contains flavonoids and other polyphenols that have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in affected blood vessels, thereby improving blood flow.

Vasodilatory properties: The cream's bioactive compounds, such as silica and saponins, can dilate blood vessels and improve blood circulation, allowing more oxygen and nutrients to reach affected areas, alleviating symptoms of PAOD.

Wound-healing and tissue repair promotion: Field Horsetail's extracts have been shown to enhance collagen synthesis, promote wound healing, and reduce the risk of complications in chronic wounds, which is particularly beneficial for patients with PAOD who may experience slow-healing wounds or ulcers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silicon and other minerals, Field Horsetail roots help improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation, making them ideal for treating peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, Field Horsetail rhyzomes have been shown to inhibit platelet aggregation and reduce oxidative stress, beneficial for patients with PAD.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality equisetum arvense leaves and stems, ensuring they are free of contaminants and pesticides.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for several hours or overnight.
Grind 20 grams of dried equisetum arvense into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix one teaspoon of the equisetum arvense powder with three tablespoons of cold-pressed coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a glass jar, pressing on the solids to extract as much cream as possible, and let it cool before storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for peripheral arterial occlusive disease are topical preparations containing plant extracts that help alleviate symptoms of PAD. These creams, often containing ingredients like Ginkgo biloba, Turmeric, and Boswellia, help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in affected areas. Examples of such creams include creams containing Ginkgo biloba and Turmeric, which have been shown to improve walking distances and reduce pain in patients with PAD, significantly improving their quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to peripheral arterial occlusive disease that are treated with herbal creams are chronic venous insufficiency, varicose veins, and Raynaud's disease. Herbal creams containing ingredients like horse chestnut extract, bilberry extract, and capsaicin have been used to alleviate symptoms such as pain, swelling, and discoloration associated with these conditions. These creams may help improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and promote healing.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with peripheral arterial occlusive disease is a blend of Ginkgo biloba, Boswellia, and Turmeric. Ginkgo biloba improves blood flow and reduces inflammation, while Boswellia eases pain and swelling. Turmeric, with its anti-inflammatory properties, helps to reduce oxidative stress and promote healing. This combination may be applied topically to affected areas, such as legs and feet, to enhance circulation and alleviate symptoms associated with peripheral arterial occlusive disease. Consult a healthcare professional before use.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Annales Academiae Medicae Stetinensis, herbal creams containing bioactive compounds like those found in PADMA-28 may be beneficial for patients with peripheral arterial occlusive disease due to intermittent claudication. The study, conducted on 50 patients with diagnosed arteriosclerosis affecting the lower extremities in stage II, found a statistically significant elongation of the claudication distance after 16 weeks of treatment with PADMA-28, while patients receiving a placebo did not show any positive results. This suggests that herbal creams may help alleviate symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease by reducing blood platelet aggregation, decreasing cholesterol and triglyceride levels, and increasing levels of beneficial lipoproteins.
